name: Construction of Projective Readout System
description: Theorem proving the construction and equivalence of a system Z2 with properly aligned projective readout to replace a given system Z1.

elements.text: If Z1 ∈ DSYSTEMS, Z2 = (SZ2, IZ2, OZ2, NZ2, RZ2) is constructed as specified, then Z2 ∈ DSYSTEMS, RZ2 is properly aligned, and Z2 behaves equivalently to Z1.
Z2 ∈ DSYSTEMS is proven by showing non-emptiness of spaces and function requirements of NZ2 and RZ2.
RZ2 is a properly aligned projective readout function.
If (f, x, t) ∈ EXZ1, then (f, y, t) ∈ EXZ2 where y is the extended state.
STZ1(f, x)(t) = PJN(n + 1, ..., n + m)(STZ2(f, y)(t)) is proved by induction on time t.
OTZ1(f, x)(t) = OTZ2(f, y)(t) is proved using state trajectory equivalence.
